subtasks.task: Determine the winner of the most recent completed match
subtasks.result:
By Iliana Limón Romero
Morocco 4, Haiti 2, final
Soufiane Rahimi passed across the penalty area to Gessime Yassine, and Yassine tapped in the ball in front an empty net in the 89th minute. The goal was upheld after VAR review.
Haiti could not score in stoppage time and closed out its World Cup run with the loss.
> A disaster at the back for Haiti and Morocco capitalize on the mistake 🇲🇦  
>   
> 20-year-old Gessime Yassine gets his FIFA World Cup moment! pic.twitter.com/TZqQcS6CAK
>
> — FOX Soccer (@FOXSoccer) June 24, 2026
Advertisement
  + Email
  + Facebook
  + X
  + LinkedIn
  + Threads
  + Reddit
  + WhatsApp
Soufiane Rahimi gives Morocco its first lead
By Iliana Limón Romero
By Iliana Limón Romero
Brazil 1, Scotland 0, 26th minute
Scotland appeared to be off to a nightmare start in Miami Gardens, Fla., but it was saved by VAR.
Vinícius Júnior pressed as Scotland worked to clear the ball, forced a turnover and scored. After VAR review, the referee determimed Júnior tapped the defender’s calf before getting the ball and was called for a foul on the play, erasing the goal.
Scotland made it to the hydration break without surrendering another goal and is sure to discuss how to handle Brazil’s press that has generated both its scoring chances.
> Vini Jr. nearly had a brace but the goal was called back after a VAR check ❌ pic.twitter.com/4FDfPMuIPt
>
> — FOX Sports (@FOXSports) June 24, 2026
> Morocco finds the EQUALIZER against Haiti 🇲🇦  
>   
> It's Achraf Hakimi pic.twitter.com/9HCNo6pOhF
>
> — FOX Soccer (@FOXSoccer) June 24, 2026

Scotland saved by VAR, avoids giving up a second goal
Brazil's Vinícius Júnior argues with the referee after his second goal was erased and he was assessed a foul. 
Brazil’s Vinícius Júnior argues with the referee after his second goal was erased and he was assessed a foul during a World Cup match against Scotland in Miami Gardens, Fla., on Wednesday.
(Michael Reaves / Getty Images)

### Who won the last World Cup?
Argentina won the last FIFA World Cup, which was held in Qatar in 2022. In a stunning high-scoring final match—in which Kylian Mbappé of France became the first player since 1966 to score three goals in a final—Argentina beat out France in the World Cup’s third shoot-out victory in its history. The winning squad was guided by its captain, Lionel Messi, who scored two goals and made a penalty kick during the shoot-out.

final_answer:
The most recent completed match in the FIFA World Cup 2026 is the semi-final match between France and Spain, which took place on July 14, 2026, at the Dallas Stadium. Spain won the match with a score of 2-0.

Important moments from this match are not provided in the given search results. However, the results do provide information on other matches, including a match between Morocco and Haiti, where Morocco won 4-2, and a match between Brazil and Scotland, where the goal scored by Vinícius Júnior was called back after a VAR check.

Since the final match has not occurred yet, the winner of the most recent completed match is Spain, who defeated France 2-0 in the semi-final.
